---
title: "2026 NBA Finals · Knicks vs Spurs"
subtitle: "A ggsql + dbt data story"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Charts"
    embed-resources: true
jupyter: ggsql
---

```{bash}
# Option A trigger: runs dbt before ggsql cells execute.
# Controlled by RUN_DBT env var so local dev (make quarto) skips it
# while Connect scheduled renders run the full pipeline.
#
#   Local:    make                        → skips this cell (RUN_DBT unset)
#   Connect:  RUN_DBT=true quarto render  → runs dbt build first
#
if [ "${RUN_DBT}" = "true" ]; then
  echo "▶ Running dbt pipeline..."
  uv run dbt build \
    --profiles-dir nba_dbt \
    --project-dir  nba_dbt
  echo "✓ dbt build complete"
fi
```

## Shot Charts — Spurs Road to the Finals

### vs Oklahoma City Thunder · Western Conference Finals

The Spurs' hardest test: OKC finished 65–17, the best record in the West.
Wembanyama logged 60 blocks across the playoffs — beaten only by Tim Duncan in 2007.

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label, category
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'San Antonio Spurs'
  AND opponent = 'Oklahoma City Thunder'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Spurs vs OKC — WCF Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

### vs Minnesota Timberwolves · Second Round

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'San Antonio Spurs'
  AND opponent = 'Minnesota Timberwolves'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Spurs vs Minnesota — R2 Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

### vs Portland Trail Blazers · First Round

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'San Antonio Spurs'
  AND opponent = 'Portland Trail Blazers'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Spurs vs Portland — R1 Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

---

## Spurs Playoff Road — Game by Game

Three rounds, eighteen games. Wembanyama and Co. dropped games in every series
but never surrendered control. The Thunder series (games 15–21) shows why Game 7
on the road felt like an upset — it was.

```{ggsql}
SELECT playoff_game_num, margin, round, opponent
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_game_scores.parquet'
WHERE team = 'San Antonio Spurs'
VISUALISE playoff_game_num AS x, margin AS y, round AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'identity'
PLACE rule
  SETTING y => 0, color => '#334155', linewidth => 0.5
LABEL title => 'Spurs — Playoff Margin Game by Game',
      x => 'Playoff Game #', y => 'Point Margin (+ = win)',
      color => 'Round'
```

---

## Shot Charts — Knicks Road to the Finals

### vs Cleveland Cavaliers · Eastern Conference Finals

The Knicks swept the #1 seed Cavaliers. Their paint dominance
(68.1% in the restricted area) shows up clearly here.

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'New York Knicks'
  AND opponent = 'Cleveland Cavaliers'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Knicks vs Cleveland — ECF Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

### vs Philadelphia 76ers · Second Round

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'New York Knicks'
  AND opponent = 'Philadelphia 76ers'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Knicks vs Philadelphia — R2 Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

### vs Atlanta Hawks · First Round

```{ggsql}
SELECT loc_x, loc_y, made_label
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_chart.parquet'
WHERE team = 'New York Knicks'
  AND opponent = 'Atlanta Hawks'
VISUALISE loc_x AS x, loc_y AS y, made_label AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING opacity => 0.65, size => 3
SCALE color
  FROM ['Made', 'Missed']
  TO ['#22c55e', '#ef4444']
LABEL title => 'Knicks vs Atlanta — R1 Shot Chart',
      x => 'Court Width', y => 'Distance from Basket',
      color => ''
```

---

## Knicks Playoff Road — Game by Game

Twelve wins, two losses — both in the first round against Atlanta before the Knicks
shifted into a different gear entirely. Every game from the second round onward was a win,
with margins that only grew.

```{ggsql}
SELECT playoff_game_num, margin, round, opponent
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_game_scores.parquet'
WHERE team = 'New York Knicks'
VISUALISE playoff_game_num AS x, margin AS y, round AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'identity'
PLACE rule
  SETTING y => 0, color => '#334155', linewidth => 0.5
LABEL title => 'Knicks — Playoff Margin Game by Game',
      x => 'Playoff Game #', y => 'Point Margin (+ = win)',
      color => 'Round'
```

---

## Shot Profile by Zone

Where each team shoots from — and how efficiently.

```{ggsql}
SELECT shot_zone, fg_pct, team
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_zones.parquet'
WHERE stat_type = 'playoffs'
ORDER BY zone_order
VISUALISE shot_zone AS x, fg_pct AS y, team AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
LABEL title => 'Playoff FG% by Shot Zone',
      x => '', y => 'Field Goal %', color => 'Team'
```

```{ggsql}
SELECT shot_zone, pct_of_fga, team
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_shot_zones.parquet'
WHERE stat_type = 'playoffs'
ORDER BY zone_order
VISUALISE shot_zone AS x, pct_of_fga AS y, team AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
LABEL title => 'Shot Distribution by Zone (% of FGA)',
      x => '', y => '% of Attempts', color => 'Team'
```

---

## Path to the Finals — Opponent Difficulty

The Spurs had the harder road. OKC at 65–17 (+11.8 net rating) was
the toughest opponent either team faced.

```{ggsql}
SELECT opponent, team, avg_margin, round
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_playoff_path.parquet'
ORDER BY team, round_num
VISUALISE opponent AS x, avg_margin AS y, team AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
PLACE rule
  SETTING y => 0, color => '#64748b', linewidth => 0.5
LABEL title => 'Average Point Margin by Series',
      x => 'Opponent', y => 'Avg Margin (positive = won by)', color => 'Team'
```

```{ggsql}
SELECT opponent, team, opp_wins, opp_net_rtg, difficulty_score
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_playoff_path.parquet'
VISUALISE opp_wins AS x, difficulty_score AS y, team AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING size => 5, opacity => 0.85
DRAW text
  MAPPING opponent AS label
  SETTING fontsize => 9
LABEL title => 'Opponent Strength — Wins vs Difficulty Score',
      x => 'Opponent Regular Season Wins', y => 'Difficulty Score', color => 'Team'
```

---

## Offense & Defense — Regular Season vs Playoffs

The Knicks held opponents to 30% from three in the playoffs — nearly five points
below their own regular-season three-point defense. The Spurs' Wembanyama effect
compressed opponents' paint attempts throughout, but the three-point line was where
the Knicks separated themselves defensively.

```{ggsql}
SELECT team, side, period, value
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_efficiency_comparison.parquet'
WHERE category = 'Scoring'
VISUALISE side AS x, value AS y, period AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
FACET team
LABEL title => 'Scoring Offense & Defense — Regular Season vs Playoffs',
      x => '', y => 'Points per Game', color => ''
```

```{ggsql}
SELECT team, side, period, value
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_efficiency_comparison.parquet'
WHERE category = '3PT Shooting'
VISUALISE side AS x, value AS y, period AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
FACET team
LABEL title => '3-Point Shooting — Regular Season vs Playoffs',
      x => '', y => '3PT% (defense = opponent %)', color => ''
```

---

## Player Comparison

```{ggsql}
SELECT player, team, playoff_ppg, playoff_apg, playoff_rpg
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_player_comparison.parquet'
VISUALISE playoff_ppg AS x, playoff_apg AS y, team AS color
DRAW point
  SETTING size => 4, opacity => 0.85
DRAW text
  MAPPING player AS label
  SETTING fontsize => 9
LABEL title => 'Playoff Scoring vs Assists',
      x => 'Points per Game', y => 'Assists per Game', color => 'Team'
```

```{ggsql}
SELECT player, team, ppg_delta
FROM 'nba_dbt/exports/mart_player_comparison.parquet'
ORDER BY team, ppg_delta DESC
VISUALISE player AS x, ppg_delta AS y, team AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'identity'
PLACE rule
  SETTING y => 0, color => '#64748b', linewidth => 0.5
LABEL title => 'Playoffs vs Regular Season — PPG Change',
      x => '', y => 'PPG Δ (+ = better in playoffs)', color => 'Team'
```

---

## Regular Season Head-to-Head

Brunson averaged 26 points across the three regular-season meetings.
The Knicks won 2 of 3, including the decisive March blowout.

```{ggsql}
SELECT context, 'New York Knicks' AS team, knicks_score AS score
FROM 'nba_dbt/seeds/raw_head_to_head.csv'
UNION ALL
SELECT context, 'San Antonio Spurs' AS team, spurs_score AS score
FROM 'nba_dbt/seeds/raw_head_to_head.csv'
VISUALISE context AS x, score AS y, team AS color
DRAW bar
  SETTING position => 'dodge'
LABEL title => 'Regular Season Head-to-Head Scores',
      x => '', y => 'Points', color => 'Team'
```
